pages.full_text:
2 hour walk in the evening.
Friday we packed up & left. & went to see Madame de Nayelle's mother's château, which she sold about 8 years ago - __Chambonais__. A fairy tale castle, with shining turrets & a beautiful garden 'les terraces' with water in a moat at one end of the garden. It is now a hotel & the gardens are overgrown. The village is one of the loveliest we've seen, very old & unspoilt. An old lady (Madam [?Lagrage]) who knows the Nayelle's well, was sweeping around the church & sold us peaches, strawberries, apple juice. The church is old, with head gargoyles, & one of a person's bum on the loo.
   Thomas bought a boat at __Joyeuse__ & we started driving down the River Ardèche (which joins the Rhone) to camp
pages.notes: Text continues from previous page (starts mid-narrative) and is cut off at the bottom mid-sentence. 'Chambonais' is underlined — could also read as 'Chambonais' or 'Chambonas' (a village near Joyeuse in Ardèche). 'Lagrage' is uncertain; could be 'Lagrave' or similar. 'Madame de Nayelle' appears to be a family acquaintance. No year is visible on this page; context (France, Ardèche) is consistent with the volume's 1989–2003 range.

pages.route_sketch_description: A pen sketch occupying most of the page. The label 'Aigueze' appears at the top-left. The upper portion shows a detailed drawing of a village skyline: a church with a tall spire on the left, rows of buildings/houses, and what appears to be castle ruins or fortified walls on the right. Below the village, there is a sketch of steep cliffs or rocky gorge walls. At the bottom, the scene shows a river with small figures of people in boats/canoes on the water, with rocks visible in the river.
pages.notes: Page is almost entirely a hand-drawn sketch of Aigueze (a village at the mouth of the Gorges de l'Ardèche where it meets the Rhône), consistent with the left-page narrative of travelling down the River Ardèche. The only text is the place label 'Aigueze' at top-left.
